name: cloudflare-blade-mcp
title: Cloudflare Edge Platform
description: Manage your Cloudflare stack — DNS, Workers, Pages, R2, KV, D1, Tunnels, and cache purge. 53 tools with token-efficient output and safety gates on all writes.
tagline: Full-stack Cloudflare management from DNS to deploy
author: groupthink-dev

highlights.text: Eight domains — DNS, Workers, Pages, R2, KV, D1, Tunnels, Cache
53 tools with full CRUD and observability
Token-efficient formatters strip 60-80% of API noise
Confirm gates on every destructive operation

readme:
Cloudflare Edge Platform gives your AI agent full operational control over your Cloudflare infrastructure. Manage DNS records, deploy Workers, monitor Pages builds, store data in KV and D1, route traffic through Tunnels, manage R2 buckets, and purge cache — all through a single MCP server.

**Token-efficient by design.** Every API response passes through a concise formatter that strips metadata noise and returns only the fields that matter. This saves 60-80% on context window usage compared to raw Cloudflare API responses.

**Safety-first writes.** All destructive operations — record deletion, secret removal, cache purge, deployment rollback — require an explicit `confirm: true` parameter. Your agent can read freely but must be intentional about writes.

**Eight integrated domains** cover the full Cloudflare platform: DNS zone and record management, Workers script deployment and versioning, Pages project observability and rollback, R2 bucket lifecycle, KV namespace storage, D1 SQLite databases, Cloudflare Tunnel routing, and edge cache purge.

env.name: CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN
env.description: Cloudflare API token with Zone DNS Edit, Workers Scripts/KV/D1/Tunnel/R2 Edit, Pages Read, Cache Purge permissions

env.name: CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID
env.description: Cloudflare Account ID — required for KV, D1, Tunnel, Workers, Pages, and R2 tools

env.name: MCP_API_TOKEN
env.description: Bearer token for HTTP transport authentication (optional, only needed for HTTP mode)

setup.blurb: Manage DNS, Workers, Pages, R2, KV, D1, Tunnels, and Cache. 53 tools with token-efficient output.
setup.help_text: Create a custom API token at dash.cloudflare.com/profile/api-tokens. Grant Zone > DNS > Edit, Account > Workers Scripts/KV/D1/Tunnel/R2 > Edit, and Zone > Cache Purge > Purge permissions. Tokens can be scoped to specific zones if you only need DNS.

fields.label: API Token
fields.help: Custom token with Zone DNS Edit and Account-level KV/D1/Tunnel Edit permissions

fields.label: Account ID
fields.help: Found on any zone's overview page in the Cloudflare dashboard, or via the API
